name: Côte d'Ivoire (Republic of Côte d'Ivoire / République de Côte d'Ivoire)
summary: Côte d'Ivoire is a West African civil-law republic whose family-law framework operates under the Family Code (Civil Code Book I, as substantially amended by Law 64-375 of 1964 and Law 83-799 of 1983, with major reform via Law 2019-573 of 2019) governing marriage, parental authority and child custody. Parental authority and child custody are governed by Civil Code arts. 17-29 (post-2019 reform). The Supreme Court (Cour suprême) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Council (Conseil constitutionnel) operates separate constitutional review. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Court of First Instance (Tribunal de Première Instance), with specialised Family-Tribunal procedure. Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health framework. Côte d'Ivoire is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the interest-of-the-child standard. Côte d'Ivoire is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.citation: Civil Code Book I (as amended Law 2019-573)
statutory_framework.title: Civil Code Book I — Marriage and Family
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Civil Code drawing on French civil-law substantive heritage. Substantively reformed 1983 (Law 83-799) and 2019 (Law 2019-573) — 2019 reform codifying gender-equal marriage, parental authority and divorce provisions.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court (Cour suprême)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Council (Conseil constitutionnel)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Council with original jurisdiction over constitutional review.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Health, Côte d'Ivoire
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Ivorian family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Côte d'Ivoire independence + Houphouët-Boigny founding-presidency
key_developments.description: Côte d'Ivoire independence 7 August 1960 from France — establishing presidential republic framework. Félix Houphouët-Boigny first president 1960-1993 substantively distinctive African extended-presidency 33-year framework. Foundational pre-1990s-multipartyism framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Civil Code Book I (Law 64-375)
key_developments.description: Federal Civil Code Book I enacted 7 October 1964 post-independence drawing on French civil-law substantive heritage. Foundational substantive-civil-law framework for contemporary family-law-jurisprudence persisting through 1983 and 2019 reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Civil Code 1983 reform (Law 83-799)
key_developments.description: Substantial reform of family-law provisions Law 83-799 of 2 August 1983 — substantive family-law-modernisation framework. Foundational pre-2019-reform framework affecting subsequent democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Côte d'Ivoire ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Côte d'Ivoire ratified the UNCRC on 4 February 1991 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 2019 Civil Code reform substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Houphouët-Boigny death + Bédié succession + Ivorian-democratic-trajectory framework
key_developments.description: Félix Houphouët-Boigny died 7 December 1993 — Henri Konan Bédié assumed presidency. Substantively distinctive African founding-presidency-succession framework. Subsequent Ivoirité ideology framework substantially affecting political-institutional-trajectory and ethnic-cohesion framework.

key_developments.title: First Ivorian Civil War 2002-2007 + UNOCI peacekeeping framework
key_developments.description: First Ivorian Civil War 19 September 2002 – 4 March 2007 between government forces and Forces Nouvelles. United Nations Operation in Côte d'Ivoire (UNOCI) peacekeeping mission 2004-2017. Substantive demographic and family-law-implementation disruption affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Second Ivorian Civil War 2010-2011 + Gbagbo-Ouattara succession + ICC framework
key_developments.description: Second Ivorian Civil War 28 November 2010 – 11 April 2011 between Laurent Gbagbo and Alassane Ouattara following disputed 2010 election. Laurent Gbagbo arrested and transferred to ICC The Hague. Substantive 21st-century African post-electoral conflict framework affecting subsequent democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Constitution 2016 + Third-Republic framework
key_developments.description: Constitution of Côte d'Ivoire 2016 adopted 30 October 2016 by referendum — substantively reforming political-institutional framework establishing Third Republic. Substantive constitutional anchor for contemporary family-law jurisprudence including reformed family-law framework.

key_developments.title: Civil Code 2019 reform (Law 2019-573) + gender-equal-marriage framework
key_developments.description: Major reform Law 2019-573 of 26 June 2019 codifying gender-equal marriage, parental authority and divorce provisions. Substantively significant family-law-modernisation within Francophone West African framework. Most-comprehensive family-law-reform within post-civil-war framework.

key_developments.title: ECOWAS Court of Justice + Conseil National des Droits de l'Homme institutional framework + AfCFTA implementation
key_developments.description: Côte d'Ivoire continuing ECOWAS Court of Justice framework participation 2023 substantively affecting cross-border family-law adjudication. Conseil National des Droits de l'Homme (CNDH) institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Law 2019-573 + Civil Code framework. African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) implementation 2021-onwards substantively significant for institutional context. Substantively significant Francophone West African institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Cour suprême + Conseil constitutionnel — interest-of-the-child substantive register + Ouattara continuing-presidency
key_developments.description: Cour suprême and Conseil constitutionnel continue to develop interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Civil Code Book I + Law 2019-573 framework in custody disputes within Alassane Ouattara presidency (since 2011 — over 13 years). Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Côte d'Ivoire operates a French-civil-law family-law framework with substantial 1964 and 1983 and 2019 reform trajectory — 2019 reform places Côte d'Ivoire among the more recent gender-equality codifications within the Francophone West African cluster.
Non-Hague Convention status places Côte d'Ivoire in the non-Hague West African cluster — structural distinction from Hague-acceding Senegal.
Civil-law framework without Islamic-law personal-status hybrid distinguishes Côte d'Ivoire from Senegal's hybrid framework within the Francophone West African cluster.
editorial_notes: Côte d'Ivoire jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full Houphouët-Boigny-to-Ouattara trajectory: 1960-Côte-d'Ivoire-independence-+-Houphouët-Boigny + 1964-Civil-Code-Book-I-Law-64-375 + 1983-Civil-Code-reform-Law-83-799 + 1991-UNCRC-ratification + 1993-Houphouët-Boigny-death-+-Bédié-+-Ivoirité-ideology + 2002-First-Ivorian-Civil-War-+-UNOCI + 2010-Second-Ivorian-Civil-War-+-Gbagbo-Ouattara-+-ICC + 2016-Constitution-+-Third-Republic + 2019-Civil-Code-reform-Law-2019-573-+-gender-equal-marriage + 2024-Cour-suprême-+-Conseil-constitutionnel-+-Ouattara-continuing-presidency.
Civil-law Francophone West Africa (French civil-law substantive heritage, 1964 → 1983 → 2019 reform trajectory). Non-Hague Convention.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive interest-of-the-child analysis under Civil Code Book I + Law 2019-573 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins West-African + Francophone + civil-law + Houphouët-Boigny-founding-presidency-33-years-1960-1993 + Ivoirité-ideology-distinctive + First-Ivorian-Civil-War-2002-2007-+-UNOCI + Second-Ivorian-Civil-War-2010-2011-+-Gbagbo-ICC-distinctive + Civil-Code-reform-2019-573-gender-equal-marriage + Constitution-2016-Third-Republic + Ouattara-continuing-presidency-13-years + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
